title: viguiera multiflora var. macrocephala
intro: Viguiera multiflora var. macrocephala, also known as Tree Marigold or Yellow Marigold, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and multiple branches. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, contributing to its numerous health benefits, including wound healing, immune system support, and antioxidant protection. Viguiera multiflora var. macrocephala is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically as a cream or ointment to treat skin conditions.
